title: potentilla tabernaemontani subvar. parviflora
intro: Potentilla tabernaemontani subvar. parviflora, also known as the Dwarf Five-finger Clover or Small-flowered Potentilla, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the rose family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Potentilla tabernaemontani subvar. parviflora include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Potentilla tabernaemontani subvar. parviflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
